data.content:
Here are **three evaluation-phase performance issues** in this project:

---

### 1. Deep Import Chain (`level1` → `level2` → `level3`)

Line 6 of `EvalHeavy.csproj` imports `level1.props`, which imports `level2.props`, which imports `level3.props`. Each import adds file I/O and evaluation overhead. MSBuild must open, parse, and evaluate every file in the chain before any build targets execute. Deep nesting (>20 levels is a red flag) makes evaluation increasingly expensive. **Flatten these into a single `.props` file** or import them directly from the `.csproj` to reduce nesting depth.

### 2. Overly Broad Glob Pattern

```xml
<AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.*" Exclude="**\bin\**;**\obj\**" />
```

The `**\*.*` glob walks the **entire directory tree** during evaluation. It will traverse directories like `node_modules`, `.git`, `packages`, and any other large subtrees — only excluding `bin` and `obj`. Glob expansion happens at evaluation time (not execution time), so this directly slows down every project load.

**Fix:** Restrict the glob to specific extensions and add `DefaultItemExcludes`:

```xml
<PropertyGroup>
  <DefaultItemExcludes>$(DefaultItemExcludes);node_modules\**;.git\**</DefaultItemExcludes>
</PropertyGroup>
<ItemGroup>
  <AdditionalFiles Include="**\*.txt;**\*.md" />
</ItemGroup>
```

### 3. Property Function Performing File I/O During Evaluation

```xml
<BuildNotes>$([System.IO.File]::ReadAllText('build-notes.txt'))</BuildNotes>
```

This calls `System.IO.File.ReadAllText()` as a **property function during the evaluation phase**. Every time MSBuild evaluates the project (IDE load, restore, build, design-time builds), it reads this file from disk synchronously. This is one of the most expensive evaluation-time patterns.

**Fix:** Move file reading to a target in the **execution phase** so it only runs when actually needed:

```xml
<Target Name="ReadBuildNotes" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <ReadLinesFromFile File="build-notes.txt">
    <Output TaskParameter="Lines" ItemName="BuildNotesLines" />
  </ReadLinesFromFile>
</Target>
```

---

### Summary

| Issue | Phase Impact | Severity |
|---|---|---|
| Deep import chain (3 levels) | Extra file I/O per evaluation | Medium |
| `**\*.*` glob | Full directory tree walk | High |
| `File::ReadAllText` property function | Disk I/O on every evaluation | High |

The key distinction: MSBuild's **evaluation phase** (parsing, imports, globs, property functions) runs on every IDE interaction and design-time build — far more often than actual builds. Moving expensive work to **execution-phase targets** and tightening globs are the highest-impact fixes.
